title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
Little John Jiggy Jag,
        He rode a penny nag,
            And went to Wigan to woo;
        When he came to a beck,
        He fell and broke his neck, - 
            Johnny, how dost thou now?

        I made him a hat,
        Of my coat-lap,
            And stockings of pearly blue.
        A hat and a feather,
        To keep out cold weather;
            So, Johnny, how dost thou now?
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXII. Love And Matrimony." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
